title: Capsule Network Research
description: Barrett Honors College thesis exploring capsule networks as an alternative to CNNs for image classification, evaluating their ability to preserve spatial hierarchies and pose information between features.
longDescription: Barrett Honors College undergraduate thesis exploring capsule networks as an alternative to convolutional neural networks for image classification. Capsule networks encode both the presence and the pose (position, orientation, scale) of a feature, using dynamic routing-by-agreement between capsule layers instead of max-pooling. The project implemented a capsule network in TensorFlow, benchmarked it against a comparable CNN on the same dataset, and evaluated how well each preserved spatial hierarchies between parts and wholes under viewpoint changes. The full thesis is published in ASU’s KEEP digital repository.

title: Astro Seed — Crop Health Classifier
description: University partnership with Astro Seed to build a machine learning model that classifies crop health from leaf imagery, helping identify plant disease early for agriculture-tech applications.
longDescription: Built in partnership with Astro Seed during university as part of an agriculture-tech research effort. Designed, trained, and evaluated a machine learning model that classifies crop health from leaf imagery to help identify nutritional deficiencies and disease early. Compared multiple architectures in TensorFlow and PyTorch for accuracy and inference-speed trade-offs toward a future field-deployable, low-power classifier. Also developed a remote data pipeline for large crop-image datasets and managed delivery through scrum. Source code is proprietary to the company partnership and is not publicly available.

title: EIT Research — Arduino Sensing Device
description: Designed and built an Arduino-based sensing device as part of Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT) research, handling signal acquisition and low-level firmware.
longDescription: Designed and built an Arduino-based sensing device as part of Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT) research. Work spanned the full stack of the device: analog signal acquisition circuitry, low-level C++ firmware for driving electrode arrays and sampling voltage, and a Python-based host-side pipeline for reconstructing impedance data into images.

title: Portfolio-Netflix (This Site)
description: A Netflix-styled personal portfolio site built with React, TypeScript, and Vite — profile selection, animated landing page, and content pages themed as a streaming catalog.
longDescription: A Netflix-styled personal portfolio site built with React, TypeScript, and Vite. Visitors pick a "profile" (recruiter or developer) the way you'd pick a Netflix profile, and the rest of the site — banner copy, project ordering, calls to action — adapts to that audience. Content lives in a typed JSON data layer decoupled from the components, deployed automatically to GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions on every push to main.
